response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly one hour daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a retiree living in Bari, I've grown increasingly anxious about the spread of fake news on social media. In my younger days, I was more of a skeptic, but now, with the world's problems mounting, I think it's essential to be vigilant. My primary school education and parents' lack of formal education might have contributed to my hesitation to question information. However, my high conscientiousness and openness levels make me want to learn more about this issue. I try to spend my daily hour on social media wisely, following reputable sources and fact-checking news. I also limit my time on platforms that frequently spread misinformation. I've even taken up knitting as a hobby to distract myself from the anxiety that fake news often induces. My low extraversion level means I prefer to engage in discussions with family and friends rather than online. I believe it's crucial to be aware of our online presence and the spread of fake content, not only for our mental health but also for the greater good. I've started to share articles and fact-checking resources with my friends and family, hoping to create a ripple effect. I've noticed that by being more mindful of the content we consume, we can all play a role in combating the spread of misinformation. I've also started to appreciate the value of traditional media and fact-checking organizations. I hope that by being more cautious and informed, I can contribute to a more responsible online community. I've even begun to appreciate the beauty of a well-crafted paintbrush stroke or the tranquility of a good novel, both of which help me to unwind from the anxiety that fake news can induce.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I've always been anxious about the spread of fake news, and as I've grown older, I've come to realize the importance of being vigilant. My education level and parents' lack of formal education might have contributed to my hesitation to question information, but my high conscientiousness and openness levels make me want to learn more about this issue. I've taken steps to manage my time on social media, following reputable sources and fact-checking news. I've also started to share articles and fact-checking resources with my friends and family, hoping to create a ripple effect. I believe that by being more mindful of the content we consume, we can all play a role in combating the spread of misinformation. I've come to appreciate the value of traditional media and fact-checking organizations.
